{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "kexinoh", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/91727108?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/kexinoh", "id": 91727108, "login": "kexinoh", "node_id": "U_kgDOBXelBA", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/kexinoh", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-42310", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": ["CWE-835"], "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-835", "name": "Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition ('Infinite Loop')"}], "description": "### Impact
An attacker can supply a malicious PDF that causes the process to hang indefinitely, consuming 100% CPU and making the application unresponsive.

### Patches
Patched version: 12.2.0.

PdfParser (introduced in Pillow 5.1.0) follows Prev pointers in PDF trailers to read cross-reference sections. If a
trailer's Prev pointer references an offset that has already been processed — either pointing to itself or forming a
longer cycle — the parser enters an infinite loop. Pillow now tracks previously processed trailer offsets and raises an
error if a cycle is detected.

### Workarounds
Use any version but the affected versions: >= 5.1.0, < 12.2.0

### References
 - Fix: https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/pull/9519 (https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/pull/9519)

title: PDF Parsing Trailer Infinite Loop (DoS)
